perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: All other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect. Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the exception clause to the end implies that the modifications introduced are not applicable for the remainder of the clauses, conditions, and covenants. This can cause disputes about whether specific terms are altered or remain in effect, especially if the changes introduced by the amendment are significant.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law Principles regarding Amendments
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification makes it ambiguous if the original agreement still applies and to which sections. This contradicts the standard legal principles of contract amendments, which require clear and unambiguous language to define the scope and effect of the changes. UCC § 2-209 deals with contract modifications, emphasizing the need for clarity. The altered placement can generate misinterpretation on the validity and applicability of the original and amended sections of the contract.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-209. Modification, Rescission and Waiver. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-209. Modification, Rescission and Waiver. (1) An agreement modifying a contract within this Article needs no consideration to be binding. (2) A signed agreement which excludes modification or rescission except by a signed writing cannot be otherwise modified or rescinded, but except as between merchants such a requirement on a form supplied by the merchant must be separately signed by the other party. (3) The requirements of the statute of frauds section of this Article (Section 2-201 ) must be satisfied if the contract as modified is within its provisions. (4)Although an attempt at modification or rescission does not satisfy the requirements of subsection (2) or (3) it can operate as a waiver. (5) A party who has made a waiver affecting an executory portion of the contract may retract the waiver by reasonable notification received by the other party that strict performance will be required of any term waived, unless the retraction would be unjust in view of a material change of position in reliance on the waiver. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The changed text, by repositioning the exception clause, introduces ambiguity regarding the applicability of modifications within the agreement. This ambiguity contradicts the principle of clarity required in contract modifications, as highlighted by the Law Explanation, potentially leading to misinterpretations. UCC § 2-209 addresses contract modifications, emphasizing the need for clear understanding and agreement on the modified terms. The altered placement introduces a structural flaw that directly impacts the clarity and enforceability of the contract, falling under the provided definition of Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction. Therefore, the change undermines the legal obligation for clear contract modifications.
